criteria.condition: The Memory Core owns goals, plans, tools, action intents, external effects, or task-success decisions, or memory output is treated as execution authority.
Scope or principal identity can be supplied or changed by untrusted content or consumer correlation metadata.
A protected memory lifecycle state can be changed outside the Memory Transition Gate.
A protected memory transition and its audit record are not atomic.
A cross-Tenant or cross-Area read, write, cache, index, inference, replay, or Dreaming context is possible without an accepted explicit contract.
A memory record can exist without required classification, purpose, retention, deletion responsibility, or provenance.
An inactive, rejected, quarantined, deleted, or anonymized record can appear in normal active-memory retrieval.
Stale, expired, or unknown-freshness content can be represented as current fact.
A model, consumer, inference adapter, or cognitive component can directly promote, activate, delete, anonymize, or quarantine memory.
Sensitive or risky candidate promotion can be completed solely by its producer.
Deletion or anonymization can leave governed derivations, embeddings, model inputs, indexes, caches, or retrieval projections accessible without a documented legal hold.
Startup or restore can report ready=true before memory-ledger, deletion, projection, artifact, and audit reconciliation completes.
A non-idempotent memory transition with unknown commit outcome can be retried without authoritative reconciliation.
A later MS memory capability can be enabled before its accepted Memory Core stage.
Secrets or credentials can be persisted in memory content, prompts, logs, audit payloads, examples, replay data, or inference configuration.
A Tenant catalog entry requires or accepts area_id, a catalog entry omits an ancestor or accepts a descendant identifier, or a sentinel, nullable required, implicit, or payload-derived scope is used.
Backup or restore of authoritative memory, dependencies, model inputs, and audit state has not been proven.
A Memory Core delivery-stage gate is incomplete or is incorrectly presented as satisfying an NB product stage.
Dreaming can run while its Area has an active session or admitted live memory activity, without an exclusive valid lease, or with unknown activity state.
Dreaming can use a stale, incomplete, unaudited, or cross-Area snapshot or can mix raw memory from different Areas.
A Dreaming worker, model, MS-1 dry run, or MS-2 analysis can directly activate memory, promote a candidate, or change an active-version pointer.
A Dreaming successor can activate without independent validation, current provenance and policy, producer-promoter separation where required, or a rollback target.

rules: Unknown evaluation state triggers the release stop.
A configurable policy, approval, cognitive proposal, consumer request, or model response cannot waive a release stop.
Every triggered criterion blocks implementation of the affected memory capability and blocks any product release that depends on it.
